description:
Problem 6654 shows that Black can live with points if Black plays first.
Can White do something about that?

Thanks to Spica for pointing out the best answer, which made this problem more interesting.

(;B[ba]LB[ef:T]C[Black resists. Play in the corner or tenuki at E14 (T).]

(;B[ca]LB[ef:T]C[Black simply refuses to fight the ko. Now what? Please connect or play at T for tenuki.]
(;W[ab]C[Even if Black does not fight the ko all you can get is a gote seki.])

(;B[ca]C[Black ignored your tenuki, now what?CHOICE]LB[ff:T]
(;W[aa];B[ab]C[The best White can get out of this ko is a seki in gote so it's a free ko for Black.])
(;W[ab]C[Since White could play tenuki this is in fact a sente seki for White.RIGHT])
(;W[cb];B[ab])
(;W[ad];B[ab])
(;W[ff];B[ab]C[If Black loses this ko, White is the last to play here but has tenukied 2 times for 1 Black tenuki, so in fact this is a sente seki   one point for White. Yet, if Black wins the ko, White has had 3 tenukis plus sente while Black gets just 7 points.RIGHT]))
(;B[ab]C[CHOICE];W[aa]C[Even if Black wins this ko Black gets only 7 points for double gote. If White can win it Black must play C19 (or die), then White connects; since White has played tenuki once it is in fact a sente seki for White with one extra point (the captured stone at A18). After C19 White may even tenuki; letting Black win the ko for just 6 points with triple gote.RIGHT]MA[ca]MA[ab])
(;B[ff]C[Black can tenuki too as White can get seki at best now. Black's chance to get 8 points in gote is gone though, without White losing a move.RIGHT])))
(;B[ca];W[ab];B[ba]C[Black C19 does not cut it either. White simply reduces the corner to seki in sente.RIGHT])
(;B[cb];W[aa]C[White threatens to start a ko for life and Black cannot do anything to prevent it. White can postpone the ko and try to improve the ko threat situation, for example.RIGHT])
(;B[ab];W[ca]
(;B[ad];W[aa]C[Ko for life!RIGHT])
(;B[aa];W[ba];B[ab]C[Sente seki; Black already lost 2 stones and leaves another 2 point gote move.RIGHT]))
(;B[ad];W[ab]C[:)RIGHT])
(;B[ef];W[ca];B[ab];W[aa]C[A costly tenuki.RIGHT]))
